summary: Iteration 8: HR ground-booking wizard + vendor-bookings state machine + mocked notification logging. BACKEND: 20 new pytest tests added in /app/backend/tests/test_vendor_bookings.py covering cities endpoint (sorted/distinct/filterable), case-insensitive listing filter, POST hours↔end_time derivation, POST auth (vendor 403, anonymous 403), PATCH state machine (vendor 'confirmed'→'vendor_accepted', 'declined'→'vendor_declined', cross-tenant vendor 403, admin confirm+notes, admin override of vendor_accept→rejected, HR cancel after confirmed, HR cannot set arbitrary status, BOOKING NOTIFICATION log line verified via tail of /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log) + 4 regression checks. FULL pytest suite: 145/145 PASSED (125 prior + 20 new). FRONTEND e2e via Playwright on REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL: HR wizard (Ground→Cricket→Bangalore→listing→modal) renders all vm-* testids, vm-book-start defaults to 18:00, vm-book-total='₹5,000' (price 2500 × 2h). Submit lands on /bookings → vendor-bookings-panel visible → first row status='Awaiting vendor' + vb-hr-cancel-<id> present. Vendor login → nav-link-vendor-bookings present → /bookings shows 11 accept/decline buttons; clicking accept transitions same booking to 'Vendor accepted · awaiting admin'. Admin login → /bookings shows vb-admin-open-<id>; opening reveals vb-admin-note + vb-admin-confirm + vb-admin-reject; entering note + confirm transitions row to 'Confirmed by PlaySphere' and renders the admin-note pill; toast 'Booking confirmed' shown.
action_items: OPTIONAL: PATCH /api/vendor-bookings/{id} as admin does NOT accept status='pending'. The spec says admin can set ANY of (pending|vendor_accepted|vendor_declined|confirmed|rejected|cancelled). Code (server.py L1991, ADMIN_STATUSES={'confirmed','rejected'}; allowed=VENDOR_STATUSES|ADMIN_STATUSES|HR_STATUSES) explicitly omits 'pending'. Add it if admins should be able to revert a booking back to pending.
OPTIONAL: notifications[] is unbounded (no max-length / no rollup). A spammy admin/vendor could grow the document past 16 MB. Consider keeping last N (e.g. 50) and archiving the rest.

OPTIONAL: 'BOOKING NOTIFICATION' is sent via logger.warning — fine for mock-email, but it floods backend.err.log. When email integration is wired in, demote to logger.info or move to a dedicated audit log file.

OPTIONAL: /api/vendor-listings/cities returns [] silently when no listings match — frontend should distinguish 'no cities available for this sport' vs 'still loading' in VendorMarket.jsx (currently the step just shows an empty grid).
OPTIONAL: server.py is now ~2600 lines. Routers/{events,vendors,bookings,players,uploads,settings}.py refactor is well overdue (also flagged iter5/6/7).

OPTIONAL: VendorBookings panel re-orders rows by latest event timestamp client-side, but the latest-notification banner reads from notifications[last]. If a status_change PATCH races with a delayed POST notification append, banner could lag. Consider returning the full document from the PATCH and replacing client-side state instead of relying on subsequent polling.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L1996-L2024 update_vendor_booking: silent state-machine — no terminal-state guard for vendor PATCH. A vendor can re-accept a booking that admin has already 'rejected' (state goes rejected→vendor_accepted). Add: if doc['status'] in ('rejected','cancelled','confirmed'): raise 409. Critical for audit trust once email is wired.
server.py L1942-L1944 fallback `hours=1, end_time=start+1h` silently rewrites caller intent if neither end_time nor hours supplied. Prefer 400 with explicit 'either hours or end_time required'.

server.py L1948 `sport = listing.get('sports', [None])[0]` — silently picks the FIRST sport from a multi-sport listing. If a ground supports cricket+football and HR picked it from the cricket filter, that intent is lost. Pass body.sport (from the wizard) and validate it ∈ listing.sports.

server.py L1979 `flt = {'vendor_id': '__none__'}` — placeholder filter to return [] when no vendor profile is found; a missing vendor profile for a logged-in vendor role should arguably be a 500 (data integrity), not a silent empty list.

VendorBookings.jsx — booking row exposes hr_email in plaintext to vendors. Acceptable per spec, but PII; once email is wired, this becomes a phishing vector (vendor sees HR's real corporate email). Consider masking or adding a 'Contact HR' button that funnels through PlaySphere.

Bookings.jsx: allows isVendor role to land on /bookings which historically was company-only — confirm the legacy <table data-testid='bookings-table'> at the top is hidden for vendor role (current code shows it empty but it still renders the heading 'TROPHY BOOKINGS' which is confusing for vendors).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the 16 critical UI assertions exercised (wizard 4 steps + modal defaults + submit + panel + HR cancel button + vendor nav link + vendor accept transition + admin override panel + admin confirm transition + admin note pill)

seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_ vendor bookings via pytest (idempotent — each test creates a fresh booking on listing[0] then transitions it). Listings auto-created in Bangalore/cricket if absent. Left in place as fixtures.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MOCKED EMAIL: every PATCH that changes status emits a BOOKING NOTIFICATION line to /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log; test_status_change_emits_log_line verifies this via tail. When real email is wired, replace the log assertion with the email-sent mock. The state machine in server.py L1990-L2027 has NO terminal-state guards on the vendor path — flagged in critical_code_review_comments as the highest-priority follow-up. /api/vendor-bookings PATCH does not currently accept admin status='pending' (deviation from spec); add to ADMIN_STATUSES if needed.
rca of the issue: No production bug. One spec deviation flagged: admin PATCH cannot set status back to 'pending' (server.py ADMIN_STATUSES set is missing 'pending'). All 20 new backend tests + full UI e2e pass.
